text: Walking through these thoughtfully constructed spaces, I feel a blend of curiosity and alienation. The glass room creates a sense of distance—separating observer from observed, perhaps reflecting my own habit of stepping back to analyze rather than engage. Inside the exhibit, vibrant colors and the bold R+D command my attention; yet the words about human research and coexistence echo deeper questions: How do I relate to these experiments, and do they reveal or conceal our nature? Immersed in abstraction and reflection, I feel both awe and a quiet tension—the paradox of being part of something while still feeling apart from it.
